spec.pageDescription: Reference documentation for the 1on1 template JSON format, methodology principles, and score weight system.

schema.title: Template JSON Schema
schema.description: The canonical schema for template export and import. Always in English as a technical standard. Use this schema to validate templates created with external AI tools before importing.

methodology.title: 1:1 Meeting Principles
methodology.intro: Effective 1:1 meetings follow a consistent structure that builds trust, surfaces blockers early, and creates accountability. These principles guide how templates should be designed.

continuity.title: Continuity over completeness
continuity.body: A good template is used every session. Keep it short enough that both participants can complete it without rushing — 8 to 12 questions is a practical ceiling.

specificity.title: Specific questions surface real signal
specificity.body: Vague questions like "How are things going?" yield vague answers. Specific questions like "What's the one thing blocking your progress this week?" surface actionable information.

balance.title: Balance retrospective and prospective
balance.body: Effective templates include both backward-looking questions (what happened, how do you feel about it) and forward-looking questions (what will you focus on, what do you need).

helpText.title: Help text reduces ambiguity
helpText.body: Each question should have help text that explains what a good answer looks like. This is especially important for rating scales — anchor the low and high ends.

weights.title: Score Weight System
weights.intro: Each question can carry a score weight that affects how much it contributes to the overall session score shown in analytics. The default weight is 1.
weights.zeroMeaning: Weight 0 means the question is unscored — it appears in the session but does not affect analytics.

examples.title: Worked examples
examples.basic: A template with three rating questions all weighted 1 contributes equally to the session score.
examples.emphasized: Increase weight to 2 or 3 for questions that signal high-importance outcomes — for example, a "blockers" question or an overall satisfaction rating.
examples.unscored: Set weight to 0 for open-ended text questions where a numeric score doesn't make sense, such as "What's on your mind this week?"

promptKit.title: DIY Prompt Kit
promptKit.intro: Paste this into Claude, ChatGPT, or any AI assistant to generate a 1on1 template tailored to your team.
